semanticCore: Warning example, chains, torture, restraint, or shying away
quranicRelevance: Exemplary warning punishment (Nakal - 'faja'alnaha nakalan'), and heavy chains of Hell (Ankal - 'inna ladayna ankalan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Warning example, deterrent punishment, exemplary caution, or lesson
Heavy chains, shackles, fetters, or binding constraints
To make a warning example of, punish severely, or deter

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنَّ لَدَيْنَا أَنْكَالًا وَجَحِيمًا (73:12): Indeed, with Us are heavy chains/shackles and Hellfire.
فَأَخَذَهُ اللَّهُ نَكَالَ الْآخِرَةِ وَالْأُولَىٰ (79:25): So God seized him with an exemplary warning punishment of the Hereafter and the first life (Pharaoh's end).
discardedNoise: Shying away from battle (Nukool) in military histories.
summaryEn: The root represents warning and constraint, detailing the heavy chains (Ankal) of Hell and historical deterrent punishments (Nakal) meant to serve as moral warnings.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر القيد والمنع الرادع، واصفاً أصفاد وقيود العذاب الأخروي (أنكالاً)، والعقوبة التاريخية المهلكة لفرعون لتكون عبرة مانعة لغيره (نكالاً).
rawText: النِّكْلُ signifies أَلَّذِى يُنْكِلُ أَقْرَانَهُ. (A, art. عض.) === See نَكَلٌ. نَكَالٌ Any punishment serving to give warning to others than the sufferer: (M, K, TA:) or that restrains the offender from repeating the offence. (Bd, ii. 6.) نَكَلٌ and 🡻نِكْلٌ: see بَدَلٌ. نَكِلَ 1 , aor. يَنْكُلُ, see 1 in art. فضل.
